pages.text: This section is for administration and debugging, not normal progression use. Core commands are $(bold)/aaft modify AllowedPlayers delete all$(), $(bold)/aaft modify AllowedPlayers delete other$(), $(bold)/aaft trust set <value>$(), $(bold)/aaft trust add <amount>$(), and $(bold)/aaft trust remove <amount>$().
Modify commands control world authorization slots. Use delete other when rotating one partner safely; reserve delete all for full access resets and explicit administrative maintenance.
Trust commands are best used for tests, balancing checks, and event setups. They let you set deterministic trust states for validating distance behavior, bonus scaling, and UI feedback.

For technical inspection use $(bold)/aaft tech trust get$() and $(bold)/aaft tech vars trust_item$(). These expose internal progression and cooldown state without changing gameplay rules by themselves.
$(italic)Best practice:$() after testing, return the world to clean progression conditions before continuing normal play. Persistent admin overrides can invalidate balancing and player expectations.
